So there is another possibility to detect small lesions just to show you what we do when we do it in VATS and in RATS so this is the small lesions and this is what we call the bouncing phenomenon so that means that you can not really detect it by vision but you can see it because
The nodule is harder than the surrounding tissue and then it bounces forth and back when you go over it.
This first one was doing by vats and this is robotic and you see how it bounces forth and back and this is the area we have to take out so we find what we have to take out even if we don't see it.
